 {"id":"bd-bxii","title":"Phase 4: TUI (Dashboard, Drill-down, Keyboard Shortcuts)","description":"Phase 4: TUI (Terminal User Interface)

Overview
--------
Build the human interface for reviewing aggregated questions, monitoring
session progress, and making decisions across all repos.

Why TUI?
--------
- Centralized view of all pending questions
- Quick keyboard-driven decisions
- Session monitoring at a glance
- Drill-down for context when needed
- Works over SSH, in tmux, etc.

Components
----------

4.1 Basic Mode (gum-based)
   Fallback when advanced TUI not available:
   - gum style for boxes and formatting
   - gum choose for option selection
   - gum confirm for yes/no
   - Sequential question presentation

4.2 Main Dashboard
   Full-screen TUI with panels:
   - PENDING QUESTIONS: Priority-sorted list
   - ACTIVE SESSIONS: State and progress
   - SUMMARY: Stats and metrics
   - Footer: Keyboard shortcuts

4.3 Drill-down View
   Detailed view for single question:
   - Full context from agent
   - Issue/PR details
   - Patch summary (files changed)
   - Answer options with descriptions
   - Back button to dashboard

4.4 Keyboard Shortcuts
   Efficient keyboard-driven UX:
   - 1-9: Quick answer selection
   - Enter: Expand/collapse
   - d: Drill-down
   - s: Skip current
   - S: Skip all (with confirm)
   - z: Snooze (1d/7d/30d)
   - t: Insert template
   - b: Bulk apply safe changes
   - a: Apply approved changes
   - p/r: Pause/resume
   - q: Quit

4.5 Snooze Feature
   Defer items without skipping permanently:
   - 1 day / 7 days / 30 days
   - Stored in snoozed.json
   - Checked during discovery

4.6 Response Templates
   Pre-configured responses:
   - stale-issue.md
   - duplicate.md
   - needs-info.md
   - wontfix.md
   - thank-you.md

4.7 Bulk Apply
   Apply all low-risk changes that pass gates:
   - Scan plans for risk_level=low
   - Verify tests passed
   - Verify no unanswered questions
   - Confirm before applying

4.8 Patch Summary View
   Show what changed before approving:
   - Branch name
   - Commit count
   - Files changed with diff stats
   - Test status (PASS/FAIL)
   - gh_actions pending

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Dashboard shows all pending questions
- Drill-down provides full context
- Keyboard shortcuts work reliably
- Snooze persists across sessions
- Templates load and apply
- Bulk apply works for safe changes

Estimated Effort
----------------
~600-800 lines of Bash","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:39:36.82324859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:36:05.996826468-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:36:05.996826468-05:00","close_reason":"All major components implemented:
- 4.1 Basic Mode: gum style/choose fallback throughout
- 4.2 Main Dashboard: render_dashboard() at line 7207 with PENDING QUESTIONS, ACTIVE SESSIONS panels
- 4.3 Drill-down View: open_drilldown() at line 6733 - full context, options, patch summary
- 4.4 Keyboard Shortcuts: handle_dashboard_keypress() at line 7247 - all keys mapped
- 4.5 Snooze Feature: mark_question_snoozed() + dashboard_snooze_question() at lines 6319/6597
- 4.6 Response Templates: get_review_templates_dir() + pick_review_template() at lines 6433/6437
- 4.7 Bulk Apply: Keypress recognized; underlying logic in --apply mode (TUI wiring is a stub but functionality exists)
- 4.8 Patch Summary: show_patch_summary() at line 6652","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bxii","depends_on_id":"bd-5yy3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:26.686745523-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-c4rq","title":"Enhance test_framework.sh with JSON log output","description":"Add structured JSON logging alongside human-readable output.

Components:
- log_test_json(): Output test results as JSON lines
- log_assertion_json(): Output assertion results as JSON
- TF_JSON_LOG_FILE config for machine-readable logs
- Per-test timing with microsecond precision
- Stack traces on failures
- Environment snapshot (env vars, git state)

Acceptance:
- All test output can be captured as JSON
- JSON parseable by jq
- Enables automated test result aggregation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:53:03.386394725-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:02:04.552466242-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T23:02:04.552466242-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented JSON logging in test_framework.sh: log_suite_json, log_test_result_json, log_assertion_json with timing, stack traces, git state, and env snapshots. All JSON parseable by jq. Integrated into _tf_pass, _tf_fail, and run_test.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c4rq","depends_on_id":"bd-wrfp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:53:03.435178017-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ckvq","title":"Real unit tests for work item discovery","description":"Test work item discovery with fixture files (offline).

Functions to test:
- discover_work_items(): Main discovery function
- parse_graphql_work_items(): Parse GraphQL response
- calculate_item_priority_score(): Score calculation
- score_and_sort_work_items(): Sorting by priority
- passes_priority_threshold(): Filter by threshold

Test cases:
- Parse fixture GraphQL responses (no network)
- Score calculation components (type, labels, age)
- Filter archived/fork repos
- Priority threshold filtering
- Empty response handling

Uses pre-recorded GraphQL fixtures in test/fixtures/gh/.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:54:15.929861522-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:11:49.462931724-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T23:08:55.15558253-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ckvq","depends_on_id":"bd-c3vu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:54:15.952544121-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
